  "percent_complete": 50,

  "order": [
    "identify-hazard",
    "stop-work",
    "cover-drain",
    "test-atmosphere",
    "issue-permit",
    "resume-work"
  ],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Hot work is being conducted directly over an uncovered sewer/drain, creating a severe explosion and fire hazard from accumulated sewer gases (methane, hydrogen sulfide)."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Issue an immediate stop-work order.",
    "Cover and seal the sewer/drain with an approved fire-resistant cover or shield.",
    "Test the atmosphere in and around the drain for flammable gases.",
    "Re-evaluate and re-authorize the hot work permit before resuming operations."
  ],
  "rationale": "Performing hot work over an unprotected sewer or drain violates basic safety sequences. Sewer gases are highly flammable and can be ignited by falling sparks or slag. The work must be stopped immediately, the drain must be covered/sealed, and the atmosphere must be verified safe before work can resume.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B Section 5.6.1",
    "OSHA 1926.352"
  ]
}
